name: From Caudiel to Altura via the Ojos Negros Greenway / Camino del Cid
name_original: Desde Caudiel hasta Altura por la Via Verde de Ojos Negros / Camino del Cid
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Caudiel are characterized by diverse landscapes, including the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, Spain's longest greenway. The region is nestled in El Alto Palancia, featuring rugged and mountainous terrain with peaks like Pico de las Palomas. The area is rich in natural features, including kermes oaks, common oaks, and maritime pines, situated at the intersection of the Espina and Espadán mountain ranges. This varied environment provides a range of cycling experiences from scenic greenways to challenging road routes. Best touring cycling routes around Caudiel The most popular touring cycling route is From Caudiel to Altura via the Ojos Negros Greenway / Camino del Cid, a 23.0 miles (37.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a historic railway line, offering scenic views and connecting Caudiel to other towns. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Caudiel Station – Fuensanta Viaducts loop from Caudiel, a difficult 30.3 miles (48.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and incorporates historical railway infrastructure like viaducts. Local touring cyclists also love the Ojos Negros Greenway – Benitandús Reservoir loop from Caudiel, a 55.3 miles (89.1 km) trail leading through the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros and past the Benitandús Reservoir, often completed in about 6 hours 17 minutes. Touring cycling around Caudiel is defined by the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, mountainous landscapes, and diverse forests of oaks and pines. The network offers options for easy rides along greenways, as well as more challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Caudiel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Caudiel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Caudiel?
A: Caudiel offers a good selection of touring cycling routes, with over 15 distinct tours available. These range in difficulty from easy to difficult, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What makes touring cycling in Caudiel unique?
A: Caudiel is uniquely positioned along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e934249583/from-caudiel-to-altura-via-the-ojos-negros-greenway-camino-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Ojos Negros&lt;/a&gt;, Spain's longest greenway, offering scenic rides on a former railway line. The region also features rugged, mountainous terrain with diverse landscapes, including kermes oaks, common oaks, and maritime pines, providing a variety of cycling experiences from gentle greenways to challenging climbs.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Caudiel?
A: Yes, Caudiel offers easy touring cycling routes, particularly sections of the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros. While the guide highlights one easy route, many parts of the greenway are generally accessible and suitable for beginners looking for a relaxed ride through scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Caudiel?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Caudiel provides several difficult routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20137318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caudiel Station – Fuensanta Viaducts loop from Caudiel&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 30.3-mile (48.8 km) path with significant elevation gain, incorporating historical railway infrastructure like viaducts. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38184922" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros Greenway – Benitandús Reservoir loop from Caudiel&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 55.3 miles (89.1 km) and features substantial climbing.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Caudiel?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available for touring cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20137318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caudiel Station – Fuensanta Viaducts loop from Caudiel&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38184922" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros Greenway – Benitandús Reservoir loop from Caudiel&lt;/a&gt; are excellent examples, allowing you to start and finish in Caudiel while exploring diverse landscapes and historical features.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes in Caudiel?
A: The region around Caudiel is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter stunning water features like the therapeutic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4785148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Baños - Montanejos Lake&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1310997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bride's Leap Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Other natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2099448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tío Juan Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5646525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrechos de Chillapájaros&lt;/a&gt; gorge, perfect for enjoying the diverse ecological environment.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural landmarks to explore while cycling near Caudiel?
A: Yes, the area offers several historical and cultural points of interest. You can visit the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2304606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Jérica&lt;/a&gt; with its beautiful buildings and steep alleys. Other notable sites include ancient Iberian and Roman remains like the Torre del Molino, the historic Convento de las Carmelitas Descalzas, and various castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/646697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almonecir Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Caudiel?
A: The Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, a central feature of cycling in Caudiel, is generally dog-friendly, making it a great option for touring cyclists with canine companions. However, always ensure your dog is on a leash and that you adhere to local regulations regarding pets in natural areas.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Caudiel?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros offers many sections that are ideal for family cycling. Its relatively flat and well-maintained paths provide a safe and enjoyable environment for riders of all ages, including children, to experience the scenic landscapes around Caudiel.

Q: What amenities are available along the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros near Caudiel?
A: The Vía Verde de Ojos Negros is a well-developed greenway, and sections near Caudiel often feature amenities such as rest stops, information panels, and occasional services in nearby towns. While specific bike-friendly cafes might vary, the route connects to villages where you can find places to refuel and relax.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Caudiel?
A: The touring cycling routes around Caudiel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the historical railway infrastructure like viaducts, and the opportunity to explore both the accessible Vía Verde and more challenging mountainous terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Caudiel?
A: Caudiel is an excellent destination for touring cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer. These seasons also showcase the region's diverse flora, including kermes oaks and maritime pines, in their full beauty.
tips: Bring a backpack and warm clothes for the tunnels and shady areas (even in summer)
The greenway gives you beautiful views of the Regajo reservoir after following the Palancia river over the cliff. It is worth making a stop along the way and observing both the swamp and the dam.

A wonderful picnic area in the forest. The waterfall of the source of the Berro river refreshes the soul and gives a little magic to the area. The northern access is a bit rough, but you can also pass the first indicator and enter through the southern access.

The greenway is made up of numerous tunnels and bridges built during the time the railway was in use to overcome the terrain and allow the track to be built on as flat a surface as possible. There are 16 tunnels and 8 bridges from Barracas, the last 75 kilometers of track. 

The use of lights, both front and rear, is recommended.
